# Codex Styler 0.2.0-beta.1 — Reliability & Creator Beta

This Beta turns companions into an independent, user-creatable package type while hardening the managed Codex lifecycle, visual adapter, diagnostics, and update channels.

Release tag: `v0.2.0-beta.1`

## Download and install

### macOS 13+ on Apple Silicon

1. Download `Codex-Styler_0.2.0-beta.1_aarch64-unsigned.dmg`.
2. Compare its SHA-256 digest with `SHA256SUMS.txt`.
3. Open the DMG and drag Codex Styler to Applications.
4. On first launch, Control-click Codex Styler and choose **Open**.

The macOS app is ad-hoc signed, does not have an Apple Developer ID signature, and is not notarized. Do not disable Gatekeeper globally.

### Windows 11 x64 — community-validation Beta

1. Download `Codex-Styler_0.2.0-beta.1_x64-unsigned-setup.exe`.
2. Compare its SHA-256 digest with `SHA256SUMS.txt`.
3. Review the GitHub build attestation, then run the per-user installer.

The Windows installer is not Authenticode-signed, so SmartScreen may show an unknown-publisher warning. Beta 1 is CI-built and structurally verified but explicitly awaits Windows 11 x64 community device evidence. Do not disable SmartScreen globally.

## Companion Studio

- Import a static image, naturally sorted image sequence, short H.264 video, or existing sprite atlas.
- Convert every input to a logical-frame timeline without reducing source resolution when extraction density changes.
- Preserve alpha or use local sampled-color cleanup with tolerance, feathering, despill, connected-subject retention, corner masks, eyedropper, and non-destructive keep/erase strokes.
- Align every frame to one shared crop, scale, and ground line.
- Calibrate pointer directions with a visual dial, exact angle, bidirectional timeline anchors, visual-change curve, reverse range, exclude range, seam checks, and jump warnings.
- Store blinks, breathing, and gestures as pose-aware idle clips instead of duplicate direction frames.
- Test real pointer movement, drag/snap placement, dynamic composer height, render cap, smoothing, and reduced motion.
- Autosave local projects with source copies, undo/redo, step reset, project reset, edit, delete, export, and re-import.

## Package and runtime changes

- Added the public `codex-styler-companion-v1` Schema and validated `.codex-styler-companion` ZIP format.
- Themes, companions, variants, and placement remain independent in `AppliedConfiguration`; a theme recommendation never overrides an explicit companion or **No companion** choice.
- Existing `frameAngles` themes remain compatible and migrate to calibrated one-frame poses in memory.
- Paged atlases target at most 48 MiB decoded RGBA area per page; the runtime keeps only the current and one neighboring page.
- Theme changes use a full revisioned update. Companion, size, position, and attachment changes update only the entity layer without flashing or reinjecting the background.
- Stale asynchronous apply responses cannot overwrite the newest selection.

## Reliability, compatibility, and diagnostics

- Added explicit Pending, Applying, Applied, Paused, Fallback, and Error feedback around the disconnected, restart-required, launching, connected, and applying lifecycle.
- Enhanced mode tries the complete semantic theme first and falls back only after an actual anchor, visibility, layer, stylesheet, or readability health failure. Codex version changes alone do not trigger fallback.
- Conservative mode always stays in the isolated background, scene, and companion layer.
- Dialogs, toasts, menus, tooltips, settings, right-panel tabs, and dynamic composers retain native stacking above the companion and background layers.
- Added local installation, process, loopback, adapter, recovery, and updater diagnostics; reports are previewed and exported manually as a redacted ZIP.
- Added automatic/custom installation-path recovery and a dedicated Windows compatibility issue form.
- Alpha may update to Beta; Beta accepts only newer Beta, RC, or stable; RC accepts RC or stable; stable never receives a prerelease.

## Automated evidence

- Theme and companion Schema, package round-trip, archive safety, license, frame/page bounds, migration, cleanup, calibration, paging, and updater-channel tests.
- Codex DOM fixtures for home, task, settings, right panel, application-root changes, overlays, and dynamic composer reconstruction.
- Browser E2E for start/apply, live companion selection, restore, and a keyboard-driven static companion project.
- English and Simplified Chinese light/dark screenshot baselines at 1320×840 and 960×680.
- Rust tests cover loopback reservation, trusted targets, install discovery, compatibility rules, update channels, and archive identifiers.

## Release evidence

- `SHA256SUMS.txt` covers both installers and the macOS updater archive.
- `Codex-Styler_0.2.0-beta.1.spdx.json` exports the dependency graph as SPDX 2.3.
- GitHub artifact attestations cover both installers; platform updater artifacts carry Tauri signatures.
- `latest.json` contains the macOS Apple Silicon and Windows x64 signed update entries.

Verify installers with GitHub CLI:

```bash
gh attestation verify Codex-Styler_0.2.0-beta.1_aarch64-unsigned.dmg \
  --repo xuhuanstudio/codex-styler

gh attestation verify Codex-Styler_0.2.0-beta.1_x64-unsigned-setup.exe \
  --repo xuhuanstudio/codex-styler
```

## Beta 1 limitations

- Windows 11 real-device install, managed restart, sleep/wake, update, restore, and uninstall evidence is required for Beta 2 and RC; CI is not treated as device evidence.
- macOS Intel, Windows ARM64, Linux, multiple entities, video backgrounds, 3D/WebGL, and arbitrary AI background segmentation are outside 0.2.
- MP4/MOV H.264 is the portable video input. Decode support ultimately depends on the system WebView; failures request conversion instead of silently continuing.
- Complex backgrounds may need manual keep/erase strokes or external preparation. Local cleanup does not claim universal automatic segmentation.
- macOS notarization and Windows Authenticode signing remain required before v1 Stable.

Codex Styler is unofficial and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by OpenAI.
